hero.subtitle: Connecting and empowering the Beninese community in Wisconsin since 2019.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.subtitle: Building a stronger Beninese community in Wisconsin
mission.description: WIBEN — the Wisconsin Beninese Community Association — was founded in 2019 in Madison, Wisconsin, with a clear purpose: to connect, support, and empower Beninese individuals and families living across Wisconsin.

unity.desc: Fostering solidarity and connection among all Beninese community members in Wisconsin.

culture.desc: Celebrating and preserving Beninese heritage, traditions, and identity.

service.title: Service
service.desc: Giving back through volunteering, mutual aid, and community support.

about.title: About WIBEN
about.hero_subtitle: Building community, preserving culture, empowering lives — since 2019.
about.history: The Wisconsin Beninese Community Association (WIBEN) was founded in 2019 by a group of Beninese immigrants who recognized the need for a formal organization to support their growing community in Wisconsin. Starting with just a handful of members in Madison, WIBEN has grown into a vibrant association with members across multiple Wisconsin cities.
about.mission: To connect, support, and empower Beninese individuals and families in Wisconsin — fostering a sense of belonging, cultural pride, and community resilience.
about.vision: A thriving, unified Beninese community in Wisconsin, fully integrated into Wisconsin life while preserving and celebrating its rich cultural heritage.
about.board_subtitle: Meet the dedicated volunteers who guide our association.
about.docs_subtitle: Transparency and governance documents for our members.

community.title: Our Community
community.hero_subtitle: A home away from home for Beninese families across Wisconsin.
community.membership_subtitle: Join the WIBEN family and be part of something meaningful.
